title: access_to_databases
text: PeakGuideR can annotate inferred neutral masses using compound databases , optionally, standard-adduct libraries. default, package includes small example databases workflow can run without downloading external files. example databases intended testing, examples vignette execution. intended comprehensive reference databases. example compound database loaded automatically compound_db = NULL. contains small set compounds neutral monoisotopic masses basic metadata. example standard-adduct library loaded automatically standards_db = NULL matrix = "HCCA". contains set matrix-specific standard-adduct entries used add standard-support evidence candidate matches. larger analyses, users can either download full PeakGuideR reference databases provide custom databases. Full reference databases can downloaded separately Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20705395 full databases distributed separately R package may contain metadata derived third-party resources. Users responsible complying licenses original data sources. downloading files, replace paths local paths files saved. downloaded databases can passed workflow:
code: dbs <- load_peakguider_databases(   compound_db_path = "~/Downloads/compound_mass_database.rds",   standards_db_path = "~/Downloads/standards_adduct_library.rds" )  compound_db <- dbs$compound_db standards_db <- dbs$standards_db res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = compound_db,   standards_db = standards_db )

title: Using_custom_databases
text: PeakGuideR can run user-provided compound databases standard-adduct libraries. allows users analyse data -house databases, project-specific compound lists, databases generated sources.

title: Using_custom_databases
text: custom compound database can supplied compound_db argument. table must data.frame must contain, minimum, numeric MonoisotopicMass column. column used match inferred neutral masses candidate compounds. Recommended columns shown . Column names case-sensitive. example, mass column must named MonoisotopicMass.
code: my_compound_db <- data.frame(   Source = c("custom", "custom"),   DB_ID = c("C001", "C002"),   Name = c("Citric acid", "L-carnitine"),   MolecularFormula = c("C6H8O7", "C7H15NO3"),   MonoisotopicMass = c(192.0270, 161.1052),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = my_compound_db )

title: Using_custom_databases
text: custom standard-adduct library can supplied standards_db argument. table optional. used add standard-support evidence candidate compound matches. table must data.frame must contain, minimum, columns adduct POLARITY. using custom databases, users responsible ensuring database content identifiers appropriate analysis comply licenses original data sources.
code: my_standards_db <- data.frame(   Master_List_NAME = c("Citric acid", "Citric acid"),   MOLECULAR_FORMULA = c("C6H8O7", "C6H8O7"),   adduct = c("[M+Na]+", "[M+K]+"),   POLARITY = c("pos", "pos"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = my_compound_db,   standards_db = my_standards_db )

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: PeakGuideR developed help interpret complex MALDI-MSI peak datasets, many detected m/z features may correspond isotopes, adducst, matrix-related signals alternative ion forms underlying molecule. IThe main idea workflos combine several proves evidence often checked separately (see peaks telling us): isotope morphology; carbon isotope-ratio support; elemental isotope-pattern support; adduct-family grouping; neutral-mass inference; database candidate matching adduct database validation. output therefore read annotation support rather final compound identification. practice, package intended help prioritise peaks candidate annotations worth inspecting validating .

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: vignette shows typical workflow starting already processed peak matrix. code chunks evaluated package checks, pkm msi_peaks replaced user’s objects. workflow can run 2 types input: PeakGuideR peak matrix object Cardinal MSI object can converted using cardinal_to_peakmatrix(). peak-matrix route, object contain least: mass: m/z values detected features; intensity: pixel--feature intensity matrix. example: Load example peak matrix:
code: library(PeakGuideR)  data("example_pkm", package = "PeakGuideR")  pkm <- example_pkm  str(pkm)

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: standard analysis, workflow can run peak matrix, ionisation mode , optionally, MALDI matrix:. Use ion_mode = "neg" negative-ion mode data. matrix argument used matrix-specific standard-adduct support. Currently, PeakGuideR includes standard-adduct support HCCA+DEA (ionic matrix); use matrix = NULL evidence layer used (recommended using another matrix). data stored Cardinal MSI object, workflow can called using cardinal_object: Internally, Cardinal objects converted peak-matrix format using cardinal_to_peakmatrix(). returned object list class peakguider_workflow: workflow stores intermediate evidence tables final summary tables: morph_results: candidate isotope relationships supported spatial morphology; cir_results: C13 isotope-ratio evidence candidate isotope pairs; eips_results: elemental isotope-pattern evidence non-carbon elements; adduct_edges: pairwise adduct relationships features; adduct_families: groups features compatible inferred neutral mass; relation_table: unified table collecting feature-feature relationships; feature_summary: one row per detected m/z feature; neutral_mass_candidates: inferred neutral masses database candidate matches.
code: library(PeakGuideR)  res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = example_pkm,   ion_mode = "pos", #or "neg" for negative ionization   matrix = "HCCA",#or NULL if it is not HCCA (until the moment, only HCCA matrix has been included in the library)   quiet=TRUE) res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = msi_peaks,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA" ) class(res) names(res)

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: feature_summary contains one row per detected m/z feature. table usually first object inspect, gives feature--feature view evidence found workflow. reports whether feature support : monoisotopic peak C13 support; C13 M+1 M+2 isotope peak; elemental isotope-pattern support; belonging adduct family; contributing inferred neutral mass. Useful columns include: idx: feature index. mz: feature m/z value. is_c13_m0: whether feature supported monoisotopic peak. c13_m1_idx: index putative C13 M+1 partner. c13_score: carbon isotope-ratio support score. has_c13_m2_support: whether C13 M+2 support detected. has_eips: whether elemental isotope-pattern support detected. eips_elements: elements supporting EIPS evidence. has_adduct_family: whether feature belongs inferred adduct family. adduct_roles: inferred adduct role roles feature. main_adduct_role: main adduct role assigned strongest adduct family. neutral_mass_consensus: inferred neutral mass associated main adduct family. feature can one type evidence. example, m/z feature may interpreted monoisotopic peak C13 support also member adduct family. C13 evidence interpreted relationship-based evidence. simple cases, PeakGuideR reports candidate monoisotopic feature (M+0) supported C13 isotopic features (M+1 M+2). chained C13 patterns, feature may involved one isotope relationship, example part M+0 -> M+1 -> M+2 series. cases flagged is_chained_c13 inspected relation_table assigning final interpretation. # Relation table relation_table useful want see individual relationships led final feature-level summary. collects relationships detected different modules common table. Useful columns include: from_idx, to_idx: feature indices connected relationship. from_mz, to_mz: m/z values connected features. relation_type: type relationship, C13, EIPS adduct relation. evidence_type: evidence layer supporting relationship. evidence_score: score associated relationship. is_valid: whether relationship passed selected threshold. group_id: group identifier, example adduct family ID. from_role, to_role: interpreted roles connected features. table useful tracing given feature assigned particular role feature_summary.
code: res$feature_summary |>   dplyr::select(     idx,     mz,     is_c13_m0,     c13_m1_idx,     c13_score,     has_c13_m2_support,     has_eips,     eips_elements,     has_adduct_family,     adduct_roles,     main_adduct_role,     neutral_mass_consensus   ) |>   head() res$relation_table |>   dplyr::select(     from_idx,     to_idx,     from_mz,     to_mz,     relation_type,     evidence_type,     evidence_score,     is_valid,     group_id,     from_role,     to_role   ) |>   head()

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: adduct_families groups pairwise adduct relationships families features compatible inferred neutral mass. object contains three components: family_summary: one row per inferred adduct family; family_members: features assigned family inferred adduct roles; family_edges: pairwise adduct relationships supporting family.
code: names(res$adduct_families) res$adduct_families$family_summary |>   head()  res$adduct_families$family_members |>   head()  res$adduct_families$family_edges |>   head()

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: relation table can also visualised feature network plot_peak_network(). plot, nodes represent m/z features edges represent relationships supported workflow, isotope, EIPS adduct relationships. default, function plots valid relationships evidence score least 0.3. threshold can increased focus stronger relationships: optional idx_focus argument can used inspect neighbourhood specific feature: visualisation exploratory. Connected features interpreted putative relationships, definitive annotations. optional idx_focus argument can used visualise neighbourhood specific feature: Useful arguments include: relation_table: relation table returned run_peakguider_workflow(). min_score: minimum evidence score required draw edge. idx_focus: optional feature index used focus network around one peak. only_valid: whether plot relationships passed validity criteria. show_edge_labels: whether show relationship labels network edges. network interpreted exploratory visualisation. Connected features necessarily definitive annotations; represent putative relationships supported evidence layers used PeakGuideR.
code: plot_peak_network(   relation_table = res$relation_table ) plot_peak_network(   relation_table = res$relation_table,   min_score = 0.5 ) plot_peak_network(   relation_table = res$relation_table,   idx_focus = 25 ) plot_peak_network(   relation_table = res$relation_table,   min_score = 0.3,   only_valid = TRUE ) plot_peak_network(   relation_table = res$relation_table,   min_score = 0.6,   idx_focus = 25,   only_valid = TRUE )

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: neutral_mass_candidates main candidate annotation table. contains one row per inferred neutral mass candidate compound match. Useful columns include: neutral_mass_id: internal neutral-mass group identifier. neutral_mass_consensus: inferred neutral mass. inferred_adducts: adducts supporting inferred neutral mass. candidate_source: source database candidate match. candidate_db_id: database identifier. candidate_name: candidate compound name. candidate_formula: candidate molecular formula. candidate_neutral_mass: neutral monoisotopic mass database. candidate_ppm_error: mass error inferred neutral mass candidate. has_standard_compound_match: whether candidate compound present standard-adduct library. has_standard_adduct_match: whether inferred adduct supported compound standard-adduct library. database match mass-based interpreted putative candidate, definitive identification.
code: res$neutral_mass_candidates |>   dplyr::select(     neutral_mass_id,     neutral_mass_consensus,     inferred_adducts,     candidate_source,     candidate_db_id,     candidate_name,     candidate_formula,     candidate_neutral_mass,     candidate_ppm_error,     has_standard_compound_match,     has_standard_adduct_match   ) |>   dplyr::filter(!is.na(candidate_name)) |>   head()

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: quick summary database standard-adduct support can obtained :
code: res$neutral_mass_candidates |>   dplyr::summarise(     n_rows = dplyr::n(),     n_neutral_masses = dplyr::n_distinct(neutral_mass_id),     n_rows_with_candidate = sum(!is.na(candidate_name)),     n_neutral_masses_with_candidate = dplyr::n_distinct(       neutral_mass_id[!is.na(candidate_name)]     ),     n_with_standard_compound_match = sum(has_standard_compound_match %in% TRUE),     n_with_standard_adduct_match = sum(has_standard_adduct_match %in% TRUE)   )

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: PeakGuideR includes small example databases testing demonstration. real analyses, users download full non-commercial databases Zenodo: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20705395 downloading files, can loaded manually replacing paths real local paths using helper function (also replace file paths local paths downloaded database files saved.: pass workflow:
code: compound_db <- readRDS("path/to/compound_mass_database") standards_db <- readRDS("path/to/adducts_database") dbs <- load_peakguider_databases(   compound_db_path = "path/to/compound_mass_database.rds",   standards_db_path = "path/to/standards_adduct_database.rds" ) res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = dbs$compound_db,   standards_db = dbs$standards_db )

title: PeakGuideR workflow
text: PeakGuideR combines multiple evidence layers support annotation. However: isotope adduct evidence prove compound identity, database matches mass-based candidates, standard-adduct support matrix- preparation-specific, definitive identification may require MS/MS, standards orthogonal validation. recommended interpretation use PeakGuideR outputs prioritise candidate annotations downstream validation.

what: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: PeakGuideR R package evidence-based annotation support spatial metabolomics peak data. package combines complementary evidence layers, including isotope morphology, carbon isotope-ratio support, elemental isotope-pattern support, adduct-family grouping, neutral-mass inference database matching. Important PeakGuideR provide definitive compound identification. outputs interpreted putative annotation evidence can guide downstream validation.

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: PeakGuideR integrates several evidence layers spatial metabolomics peak annotation:

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: PeakGuideR can installed GitHub. Keep build_vignettes = TRUE install package vignettes: package vignettes can opened :
code: remotes::install_github(   "nadiamolto/PeakGuideR",   build_vignettes = TRUE,   dependencies = TRUE ) browseVignettes("PeakGuideR")

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features

code: res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = MSImagingExperimentObject,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA" ) class(res) names(res)

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
code: res$neutral_mass_candidates |>   dplyr::summarise(     n_rows = dplyr::n(),     n_neutral_masses = dplyr::n_distinct(neutral_mass_id),     n_rows_with_candidate = sum(!is.na(candidate_name)),     n_neutral_masses_with_candidate = dplyr::n_distinct(       neutral_mass_id[!is.na(candidate_name)]     ),     n_with_standard_compound_match = sum(has_standard_compound_match %in% TRUE),     n_with_standard_adduct_match = sum(has_standard_adduct_match %in% TRUE)   )

text: downloading files Zenodo, load R: pass workflow: Alternatively, available installed version, use:
code: compound_db <- readRDS("path/to/compound_mass_database_noncommercial.rds") standards_db <- readRDS("path/to/standards_adduct_library_noncommercial.rds") res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = compound_db,   standards_db = standards_db ) dbs <- load_peakguider_databases(   compound_db_path = "path/to/compound_mass_database_noncommercial.rds",   standards_db_path = "path/to/standards_adduct_library_noncommercial.rds" )  res <- run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm = pkm,   ion_mode = "pos",   matrix = "HCCA",   compound_db = dbs$compound_db,   standards_db = dbs$standards_db )

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: Standard-adduct support currently matrix-specific. matrix = "HCCA", PeakGuideR can check whether candidate compound inferred adduct present HCCA standard-adduct library. context, HCCA refers alpha-cyano-4-hydroxycinnamic acid. standard-adduct library generated using HCCA-based solid ionic matrix combined N,N-diethylaniline (DEA) deposited low-temperature thermal evaporation. Interpretation note Standard-adduct support interpreted matrix- preparation-specific evidence, universal adduct evidence MALDI matrices deposition protocols.

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: PeakGuideR outputs evidence layers annotation support. database match neutral_mass_candidates means inferred neutral mass compatible candidate compound within selected ppm tolerance. constitute definitive compound identification. Experimental validation, MS/MS, standards orthogonal evidence may required confident identification.

title: Evidence-Based Annotation of MALDI-MSI Metabolites Features
text: PeakGuideR includes small example compound standard-adduct databases testing demonstration. Full annotation databases distributed separately non-commercial research use. datasets include records derived ChEBI, NORMAN HMDB. ChEBI NORMAN/SusDat records distributed CC 4.0 terms. HMDB-derived records subject CC -NC 4.0 non-commercial use restrictions according original HMDB licensing terms. databases used retrieve putative mass-matched candidates constitute compound identification. Users responsible ensuring use complies original providers’ licenses.

title: PeakGuideR — PeakGuideR-package
text: @keywords internal @importFrom stats approx @importFrom utils data

title: PeakGuideR — PeakGuideR-package
text: Maintainer: Nadia Molto nadia.molto@gmail.com (ORCID) Authors: Nadia Molto nadia.molto@gmail.com (ORCID)

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
text: Detects pairwise adduct candidates combining: expected m/z differences adduct species spatial/intensity similarity features agreement inferred neutral masses. function returns candidate adduct edges can later grouped larger ion families pseudo-compounds.

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
code: adduct_candidates(   pkm,   ion_mode = c("pos", "neg"),   adducts = NULL,   feature_idx = NULL,   tol_ppm = 5,   neutral_tol_ppm = 5,   method = c("pearson", "cosine", "spearman"),   transform = c("none", "log1p", "zscore"),   min_quantile = 0.01,   clip_negatives = TRUE,   min_score_spatial = 0.5 )

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
text: pkm list : mass: numeric vector m/z values. intensity: numeric matrix pixels features. ion_mode Ionization mode. Either "pos" "neg". adducts Optional data-frame adduct definitions. NULL, default_adducts() used (adducts usually found MALDI-MSI). feature_idx Optional integer vector feature indices evaluate. NULL, features used. tol_ppm Numeric. PPM tolerance used search candidate peak pairs. Default 5. neutral_tol_ppm Numeric. PPM tolerance used score agreement inferred neutral masses. Default 5. value chosen high-resolution Orbitrap data adjusted instruments preprocessing settings. method Similarity metric feature intensity profiles: "pearson", "cosine" "spearman". Default "pearson". transform Intensity transformation similarity calculation: "none", "log1p" "zscore". Default "none". min_quantile Numeric range 0 1. Feature-wise low-intensity quantile filter applied retaining co-detected pixels. Pixels kept features respective threshold. Default 0.01. clip_negatives Logical. TRUE, negative intensities truncated zero transformation. Default TRUE. min_score_spatial Numeric range 0 1. Minimum spatial score required keep adduct edge. Default 0.5.

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
text: data.frame one row per candidate adduct relation: idx_i, mz_i: first feature index m/z idx_j, mz_j: second feature index m/z adduct_i, adduct_j: adduct hypotheses delta_theo, delta_obs, delta_err_ppm neutral_mass_i, neutral_mass_j, neutral_mass_mean neutral_err_ppm score_spatial, score_mass, score_adduct is_valid_adduct

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
text: function assumes mass column adduct table stores net m/z shift relative neutral mass: $$mz = M + shift$$ neutral mass recovered : $$M = mz - shift$$ pair adducts within selected ionization mode, expected m/z difference : $$\Delta m/z = shift_b - shift_a$$ Candidate feature pairs matching difference evaluated using spatial/intensity similarity neutral-mass consistency. reported score_adduct corresponds spatial similarity score filtering candidate pairs expected adduct m/z difference. neutral-mass consistency score returned score_mass inspection downstream filtering.

title: Detects adduct pairwise candidates — adduct_candidates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ adduct_res <- adduct_candidates(   pkm,   ion_mode = "pos" )  extra_adducts <- rbind(   default_adducts(),   data.frame(     name = "[M-H]-",     mode = "neg",     mass = -1.007276,     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) )  adduct_res_neg <- adduct_candidates(   pkm,   ion_mode = "neg",   adducts = extra_adducts ) } # }

what: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
title: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
text: Groups adduct candidate edges putative adduct families using pairwise output adduct_candidates(). contrast simple graph-based grouping, function groups edges inferred neutral mass, alternative adduct hypotheses involving feature kept separate neutral-mass families rather forced connected component. function returns: family-level summary table (family_summary) feature-level membership table (family_members) edge-level table family assignment (family_edges) intended provide chemically interpretable adduct families, family represents candidate neutral mass supported one mass-compatible spatially correlated adduct relationships.

title: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
code: adduct_families(   adduct_edges,   min_score_adduct = 0.3,   neutral_cluster_ppm = 5,   min_family_size = 2L,   use_only_valid = TRUE )

title: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
text: adduct_edges data.frame produced adduct_candidates(). Must contain least: idx_i, idx_j, mz_i, mz_j, score_adduct, neutral_mass_i, neutral_mass_j, neutral_mass_mean, adduct_i, adduct_j, is_valid_adduct. min_score_adduct Numeric range 0 1. Minimum adduct score required include edge. Default 0.2. neutral_cluster_ppm Numeric. PPM tolerance used cluster edges neutral-mass families. Default 5. min_family_size Integer. Minimum number unique features required keep family. Default 2L. use_only_valid Logical. TRUE, edges is_valid_adduct == TRUE used. Default TRUE.

title: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
text: list : family_summary: one row per neutral-mass adduct family family_members: one row per feature/adduct role within family family_edges: one row per adduct edge assigned family family_summary contains: family_id neutral_mass_consensus neutral_mass_range_ppm family_size n_edges mean_score_adduct median_score_adduct adducts feature_idx has_role_conflict family_members contains: family_id idx mz adduct neutral_mass_from_feature n_edges mean_edge_score family_edges contains filtered adduct candidate edges plus: family_id

title: Build neutral-mass-based adduct families from pairwise adduct candidate edges — adduct_families
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ adduct_res <- adduct_candidates(pkm, ion_mode = "pos")  adduct_fam <- adduct_families(   adduct_res,   min_score_adduct = 0.3,   neutral_cluster_ppm = 5 )  head(adduct_fam$family_summary) head(adduct_fam$family_members) head(adduct_fam$family_edges) } # }

what: Build feature-level summary from PeakGuideR relations — build_feature_summary
title: Build feature-level summary from PeakGuideR relations — build_feature_summary
text: Builds feature-level summary PeakGuideR relation table. output contains one row per feature summarizes whether peak involved isotope, EIPS adduct relationships.

title: Build feature-level summary from PeakGuideR relations — build_feature_summary
code: build_feature_summary(relation_table, adduct_fam = NULL, pkm = NULL)

title: Build feature-level summary from PeakGuideR relations — build_feature_summary
text: relation_table Output build_relation_table(). adduct_fam Optional output adduct_families(). pkm Optional peak matrix object mass. supplied, features pkm$mass included. NULL, features present relation_table returned.

title: Build feature-level summary from PeakGuideR relations — build_feature_summary
text: data.frame one row per feature.

title: Build neutral-mass candidate table — build_neutral_mass_candidates
text: Builds neutral-mass candidate table adduct families. function summarizes neutral masses inferred adduct families, adds isotope EIPS support feature_summary, matches inferred neutral masses compound mass database, optionally checks whether candidate compound inferred adduct supported standard-adduct library. output contains one row per inferred neutral mass compound candidate. Candidate compounds putative mass matches, definitive identifications.

title: Build neutral-mass candidate table — build_neutral_mass_candidates
code: build_neutral_mass_candidates(   adduct_fam,   feature_summary,   compound_db = NULL,   standards_db = NULL,   ion_mode = c("pos", "neg"),   matrix = NULL,   ppm_tol = 5,   neutral_cluster_ppm = 5,   top_n = 10L,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Build neutral-mass candidate table — build_neutral_mass_candidates
text: adduct_fam Output adduct_families(). feature_summary Output build_feature_summary(). compound_db Compound mass database. NULL, included non-commercial compound mass database loaded. standards_db Standard adduct library. NULL matrix = "HCCA", included non-commercial standard adduct library loaded. ion_mode "pos" "neg". matrix Matrix name. Standard-adduct support currently applied matrix = "HCCA". Use NULL skip standard-adduct support. ppm_tol PPM tolerance compound mass matching. neutral_cluster_ppm PPM tolerance used group similar neutral masses inferred different adduct families. top_n Maximum number compound candidates kept per neutral mass. quiet Logical. FALSE, database loading functions may print notices.

title: Build neutral-mass candidate table — build_neutral_mass_candidates
text: data.frame one row per neutral mass compound candidate.

what: Build a unified peak relation table — build_relation_table
title: Build a unified peak relation table — build_relation_table
text: Builds unified table peak--peak relationships detected PeakGuideR. table integrates internal evidence C13 isotope validation, C13 M+2 support, elemental isotope-pattern support adduct-family relationships. relation keeps original score evidence layer generated . Therefore, evidence_score interpreted together score_type.

title: Build a unified peak relation table — build_relation_table
code: build_relation_table(   cir_results = NULL,   eips_results = NULL,   adduct_fam = NULL,   only_valid = TRUE )

title: Build a unified peak relation table — build_relation_table
text: cir_results Output cir_score(). eips_results Output eips_score(). adduct_fam Output adduct_families(). only_valid Logical. TRUE, keeps valid relations.

title: Build a unified peak relation table — build_relation_table
text: data.frame one row per peak--peak relation.

what: Convert a Cardinal MSImagingExperiment object to a peak matrix — cardinal_to_peakmatrix
title: Convert a Cardinal MSImagingExperiment object to a peak matrix — cardinal_to_peakmatrix
text: Converts supported Cardinal MSI object PeakGuideR peak matrix rMSIprocPeakMatrix-like structure.

title: Convert a Cardinal MSImagingExperiment object to a peak matrix — cardinal_to_peakmatrix
code: cardinal_to_peakmatrix(   x,   value = NULL,   dataset_name = NULL,   snr = NULL,   area = NULL )

R_theo Expected theoretical isotope ratio given element atom count.

title: Score elemental isotope-pattern candidates — eips_score
text: Evaluates non-carbon isotope-pattern candidates comparing observed isotope ratio theoretical element-count ratios N, O, S, Cl Br. function can optionally add formula support comparing inferred neutral masses database-derived EIPS lookup table. Two optional reliability gates can applied: N/O/S, EIPS evaluated idx_M0 CIR-validated C13_M1 isotope. EIPS evaluated pairs already detected C13_M2 idx_M0 CIR-validated C13_M1. low-abundance N/O/S isotope channels, function can require independent C13 support monoisotopic feature. reduces spurious elemental isotope assignments noisy MSI peak tables. addition, inferred atom counts (n_hat) bounded maximum number atoms observed database element. Candidates exceeding bound discarded final selection. Expected isotope peak order elemental channel evaluated . N evaluated M+1; O, S, Cl Br evaluated M+2. EIPS perform initial mass-difference search. evaluates elemental isotope candidates already detected iso_morphology_candidates().

title: Score elemental isotope-pattern candidates — eips_score
code: eips_score(   result,   pkm,   eips_n_table = NULL,   eips_table = NULL,   ion_mode = c("pos", "neg"),   adducts = NULL,   min_score_final = 0.6,   eips_rel_tol = 0.3,   ratio_method = c("sum", "median", "mean"),   min_quantile = 0.01,   ppm_neutral = 5,   n_window = 1L,   top_n_hat = 5L,   cir_df = NULL,   morph_df = NULL,   require_c13_for = c("N", "O", "S"),   exclude_c13_m2 = TRUE,   return_debug = FALSE )

title: Score elemental isotope-pattern candidates — eips_score
text: result Output iso_morphology_candidates() (must contain least idx_M0, idx_cand, score_final, element, iso_type). pkm Peak matrix list mass intensity. eips_n_table Optional precomputed theoretical lookup table columns element, k, delta, n R_theo. NULL, internal PeakGuideR eips_n_table object used. eips_table Optional database-derived lookup table columns mz_mono, element n_el. NULL, internal PeakGuideR eips_table object used. ion_mode "pos" "neg" (used formula support enabled). adducts Optional data.frame adduct definitions. NULL, default_adducts() used. table must contain name, mode mass, mass net m/z shift relative neutral mass. min_score_final Minimum morphology score required consider pair. eips_rel_tol Relative tolerance used validity threshold ratio score scaling. ratio_method "sum", "mean", "median" aggregation isotope ratios. "sum" computes sum(I_iso) / sum(I_M0) selected pixels recommended MSI isotope-ratio estimation. min_quantile Quantile used intensity masking. Default= 0.01. ppm_neutral Ppm tolerance used evaluating neutral mass consistency elemental isotope-pattern support. n_window Allowed deviation inferred n_hat formula n_el formula support evaluated. top_n_hat Integer. Keep top-k best n_hat candidates relative error. cir_df Optional CIR results table. Must contain idx_M0 is_valid_c13. supplied, enables N/O/S gate. morph_df Optional morphology table (typically object passed result). Must contain idx_M0, idx_cand, iso_type. supplied, enables exclusion C13_M2 pairs. require_c13_for Character vector elements CIR-validated C13_M1 required. Default: c("N","O","S"). exclude_c13_m2 Logical. TRUE, excludes pairs already detected C13_M2 idx_M0 values CIR-valid C13_M1. return_debug Logical. TRUE, returns list final output internal top-n candidates.

title: Score elemental isotope-pattern candidates — eips_score
text: default, single data.frame / tibble one row per evaluated pair: idx_M0, mz_M0, idx_cand, mz_iso iso_type, element, k, delta R_obs, n_hat, R_theo_hat, eips_rel_err is_valid_eips, score_eips has_formula_support, adduct_name, neutral_mass, mz_mono_db, n_el_db, neutral_err_ppm return_debug = TRUE, returns list : eips_validation top_n_candidates

what: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
text: Adds continuous mass-accuracy score isotopic pairs detected iso_morphology_candidates(). search new candidates: scores already-detected pairs using mass error (mass_err_da / mass_err_ppm). useful morphology step uses hard tolerance window (pass/fail), still want smooth penalty rank candidates close theoretical delta.

title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
code: iso_mass_deviation_score(   pairs,   tol_ppm = 5,   tol_da = NULL,   use = c("ppm", "da"),   kernel = c("gaussian", "linear"),   combine = TRUE,   w_morph = 0.8 )

title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
text: pairs Data.frame output iso_morphology_candidates(). Must contain score_final either mass_err_ppm mass_err_da. tol_ppm Numeric. PPM tolerance used reference window. Default 5. (Typically tol_ppm used iso_morphology_candidates.) tol_da Numeric. Da tolerance used scoring Da (used use="da"). use Character: "ppm" (default) "da" decide error column use. kernel Character: "gaussian" (default) "linear". combine Logical. TRUE, compute score_combined. Default TRUE. w_morph Numeric range 0 1. Weight morphology combining. Default 0.8.

title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
text: input pairs additional columns: mass_dev_err: absolute mass error chosen units (ppm Da) mass_dev_score: continuous score range 0 1. score_combined: optional combined score

title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
text: function expects pairs contain mass_err_ppm (preferred) mass_err_da. score computed either: Gaussian (default): $$score = exp(-(err^2)/(2*sigma^2))$$ sigma = tol/2. Linear: $$score = pmax(0, 1 - |err|/tol)$$ Optionally, combined score can produced: $$score\_combined = w\_morph * score\_final + (1-w\_morph) * mass\_dev\_score$$

title: Mass deviation scoring for isotopic candidate pairs — iso_mass_deviation_score
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ pairs <- iso_morphology_candidates(pm, tol_ppm=5) pairs2 <- iso_mass_deviation_score(pairs, tol_ppm=5, kernel="gaussian", w_morph=0.8) head(pairs2) } # }

what: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
text: Detects potential isotopic relationships peaks Mass Spectrometry Imaging (MSI) peak matrix based expected mass differences spatial colocalization (correlation). computes correlation-like morphology metrics (Pearson, cosine, Spearman), optionally evaluates spatial consistency across tiles, returns isotopic pairs satisfy mass tolerance minimum similarity threshold.

title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
code: iso_morphology_candidates(   pkm,   prefer_mode = c("ppm", "dp"),   tol_ppm = 5,   tol_dp = 3L,   method = c("pearson", "cosine", "spearman"),   transform = c("none", "log1p", "zscore"),   min_quantile = 0.01,   clip_negatives = TRUE,   use_tiles = TRUE,   tiles = 9L,   tile_min_pixels = 50L,   tile_blend = c("median", "p25", "pass_rate"),   tile_alpha = 0.8,   tile_threshold = 0.8,   min_score_keep = 0.2 )

title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
text: pkm list : mass: numeric vector m/z values. intensity: matrix pixel x feature. optional pos: data.frame/matrix x y coordinates pixels (without , tile strategy disabled info message). Position information recommended get reliable results. prefer_mode "ppm" (default) "dp". Tolerance mode. tol_ppm Numeric, mass window (±ppm). Default 5. tol_dp Integer, window indices prefer_mode="dp". Default 3L. method Character, morphology metric: "pearson" (default), "cosine", "spearman". transform One "none", "log1p", "zscore". Default "none". min_quantile Numeric range 0 1, pooled lower quantile filter. Default 0.01. clip_negatives Logical, clip negative values transform. Default TRUE. use_tiles Logical, enable spatial tile evaluation pkm$pos exists. Default TRUE. tiles Integer, approximate number subregions. Default 9L. tile_min_pixels Integer, minimum pixels per tile. Default 50L. tile_blend One "median", "p25", "pass_rate". Default "median". tile_alpha Numeric range 0 1, blending global tile score. Default 0.8. tile_threshold Numeric range 0 1, threshold "pass_rate". Default 0.8. min_score_keep Numeric range 0 1, score threshold. Default 0.2.

title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
text: data.frame : idx_M0, mz_M0: anchor index m/z. iso_type: isotope type (C13_M1, C13_M2, S34, Cl37, Br81, O18,15N). element: (C.N,O,S,Cl,Br) k: isotope level (1 2). z: charge state (always 1). idx_cand, mz_cand: candidate index m/z. score_global, tile_summary, tile_sd, tile_consistency, score_final. mass_err_da, mass_err_ppm: candidate error vs theoretical mass. mass dev_err, mass_dev_score: absolute mass error corresponding mass deviation score.

title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
text: Charge state fixed z = 1 throughout method (user control). monoisotopic feature (M+0), function searches isotopic candidates theoretical mass differences z = 1: 13C series: M+1 = 1.003355, M+2 = 2 × 1.003355 isotopes (M+X level): 34S = 1.99580, 37Cl = 1.99705, 81Br = 1.99795, 18O = 2.004245 Tolerance modes: "ppm" (default): find candidates within ±tol_ppm ppm around theoretical mass. "dp": find candidates within ±tol_dp data points ordered mass axis. Preprocessing steps per intensity pair: Keep pixels features detected (x!=0 & y!=0) #' 2. Apply feature-wise low-intensity quantile filters retained pixels. Pixels kept features respective threshold. Optionally clip negatives (clip_negatives=TRUE). Apply intensity transform (none, log1p, zscore). Scoring metrics considered setting transformation method: zscore recommended pearson correlation, log1p none cosine similarity none spearman. Normalization can applied (e.g. TIC) considered regarding result interpretation. Scoring metrics (method): "pearson": squared Pearson correlation (R²), 0 1. "cosine": cosine similarity. "spearman": squared Spearman rank correlation (negatives set 0). Tile mode: use_tiles=TRUE pixel coordinates present (pkm$pos$x,y), sample split approximately sqrt(tiles)×sqrt(tiles) subregions. tile, correlation computed, summarizing via: "median": median tile scores, "p25": 25th percentile (robust lower-bound), "pass_rate": fraction tiles score ≥ tile_threshold. consistency factor computed : $$ tile\_consistency = \max(0, 1 - sd(tile\_scores)) $$ final score blends global morphology score tile-consistency factor: $$score\_final = \alpha \cdot score\_global + (1-\alpha) \cdot tile\_consistency$$ tile_alpha parameter controls contribution global score relative tile-consistency component. selected tile_blend summary returned tile_summary inspection. Output policy: Returns isotopic candidate pairs (M+0... M+k) fall within mass tolerance score_final ≥ min_score_keep. dataframe many rows isotopes found.

title: Detect isotope candidates using mass differences and ion-image morphology — iso_morphology_candidates
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ res <- iso_morphology_candidates(   pkm,   prefer_mode   = "ppm",   tol_ppm       = 5,   method        = "pearson",   transform     = "log1p",   use_tiles     = TRUE,   tiles         = 9L,   tile_blend    = "median",   tile_alpha    = 0.8,   min_score_keep= 0.2 ) head(res) } # }

what: Run the PeakGuideR annotation workflow — run_peakguider_workflow
title: Run the PeakGuideR annotation workflow — run_peakguider_workflow
text: Runs main PeakGuideR workflow either PeakGuideR peak matrix object Cardinal MSI object. workflow includes isotope morphology detection, carbon isotope-ratio validation, elemental isotope-pattern support, adduct candidate detection, adduct-family grouping, relation-table construction, feature-level summarization neutral-mass candidate matching.

title: Run the PeakGuideR annotation workflow — run_peakguider_workflow
code: run_peakguider_workflow(   pkm,   ion_mode = c("pos", "neg"),   matrix = NULL,   adducts = NULL,   compound_db = NULL,   standards_db = NULL,   morph_prefer_mode = c("ppm", "dp"),   morph_method = c("pearson", "cosine", "spearman"),   morph_transform = c("none", "log1p", "zscore"),   morph_tile_blend = c("median", "p25", "pass_rate"),   iso_min_score = 0.6,   cir_rel_tol = 0.3,   eips_rel_tol = 0.3,   ratio_method = c("sum", "mean", "median"),   adduct_tol_ppm = 5,   adduct_neutral_tol_ppm = 5,   adduct_method = c("pearson", "cosine", "spearman"),   adduct_transform = c("none", "log1p", "zscore"),   adduct_min_quantile = 0.01,   adduct_clip_negatives = TRUE,   adduct_min_score = 0.5,   neutral_cluster_ppm = 5,   candidate_ppm_tol = 5,   top_n = 10L,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Run the PeakGuideR annotation workflow — run_peakguider_workflow
text: pkm rMSI2 peak matrix object, supported Cardinal MSImagingExperiment object. Cardinal objects converted internally using cardinal_to_peakmatrix(). ion_mode Ion mode, either "pos" "neg". matrix Matrix name. Use "HCCA" enable HCCA-specific standard adduct support neutral-mass candidate matching. adducts Optional adduct definition table. NULL, PeakGuideR uses default_adducts(ion_mode). Users can inspect modify default adduct table default_adducts(). compound_db Optional compound mass database. standards_db Optional standard adduct library. morph_prefer_mode Mass-deviation preference mode used iso_morphology_candidates(), either "ppm" "dp". morph_method Spatial similarity method used iso_morphology_candidates(). morph_transform Intensity transformation used iso_morphology_candidates(). morph_tile_blend Method used combine tile-level morphology scores. iso_min_score Minimum isotope morphology score used CIR/EIPS input. cir_rel_tol Relative tolerance carbon isotope-ratio validation. eips_rel_tol Relative tolerance EIPS validation. ratio_method Ratio aggregation method. adduct_tol_ppm PPM tolerance adduct candidate detection. adduct_neutral_tol_ppm PPM tolerance adduct neutral-mass consistency. adduct_method Spatial similarity method used adduct detection. adduct_transform Intensity transformation used adduct detection. adduct_min_quantile Minimum quantile used adduct spatial vectors. adduct_clip_negatives Logical. TRUE, negative transformed values clipped adduct detection. adduct_min_score Minimum spatial score adduct candidates/families. neutral_cluster_ppm PPM tolerance used cluster inferred neutral masses. candidate_ppm_tol PPM tolerance compound candidate matching. top_n Maximum number compound candidates per neutral mass. quiet Logical. FALSE, prints progress messages.

title: Run the PeakGuideR annotation workflow — run_peakguider_workflow
text: list main PeakGuideR workflow outputs.
